title: DEBT DISCOVERY
blurb: Debt isn't a monster — but some kinds bite! Today you'll learn which debts help you and which ones hurt.
mission: Learn what debt is and how to tell good debt from bad debt.
what: Debt is money you borrow now and promise to pay back later.
key: Good debt helps you grow; bad debt just drains you.
swapGood: “I borrowed $20 for a bike to do paid deliveries.”
intro: See if a debt helps or hurts…
rule: Only borrow if it helps you and you can pay it back.
star: Smart borrowing builds you up, not down.
goal: “Only borrow $20 if it earns me more than it costs.”
smartTip: Good debt has a plan; bad debt has none!
gbBody: Some debts help you earn or learn; others just cost you.
tip: Ask 'does this help me grow?' before borrowing.
badWarn: Bad debt grows fast and traps your money!
goodNote: Smart borrowing can help you grow!

title: PAYMENT PLAN
blurb: Got more than one debt? There's a smart order to crush them. Today you'll pick your winning strategy!
mission: Learn two payoff methods: snowball (smallest first) and avalanche (highest rate first).
what: A payoff plan is the smart order you pay debts off, one at a time.
key: Attack one debt hard while paying the rest the minimum.
swapGood: “I'll crush my $10 debt first, then roll that money up.”
intro: Choose how to attack your debts…
rule: Pay the minimum on all, then attack ONE debt hard.
star: One debt down gives you momentum to keep going.
goal: “Pay off my $10 debt first, then roll that money to the next.”
smartTip: Snowball feels great; avalanche saves the most!
gbBody: A clear plan crushes debt fast; scattering payments stalls you.
gbKey: Attack one debt at a time.
tip: Roll each finished payment onto the next debt.
badWarn: Spreading payments thin keeps debt around forever!
goodNote: A focused plan gets you debt-free faster!

title: INTEREST IMPACT
blurb: Wait too long and debt grows all by itself! Today you'll see how interest piles on — and how to beat it.
mission: Learn how interest makes unpaid debt grow bigger over time.
what: Interest is extra money you owe just for borrowing over time.
key: The longer you wait, the more interest piles on top.
swapGood: “I owe $20 plus $2 interest — I'll pay it this month.”
rule: Pay early and you pay way less interest.
star: Paying fast means interest shrinks, not stacks.
goal: “Pay back my $20 fast so I only owe $2 extra, not $10.”
smartTip: Time is the secret — pay early and win!
gbBody: Pay fast and interest stays tiny; wait and it balloons.
gbKey: The sooner you pay, the less you owe.
tip: Always pay more than the minimum when you can.
badWarn: Waiting lets interest grow your debt bigger and bigger!
goodNote: Fast payers keep way more of their money!

title: REBUILDING STATION
blurb: Paid off your debt? Now rebuild! Today you'll learn to restore your money health and grow steady again.
mission: Learn how to restore your financial health after paying off debt.
what: Rebuilding is restoring your money health step by step after debt.
key: Save a little, stay steady, and your money gets strong again.
swapGood: “I'm debt-free — now I'll build a $20 safety cushion.”
rule: After debt, save first before you spend big.
star: Small steady savings rebuild your money strength.
goal: “Build a $20 safety cushion before buying anything fun.”
smartTip: A safety cushion keeps debt from coming back!
gbBody: Some habits rebuild your money; others slide you back into debt.
tip: Put savings away the day you get paid.
badWarn: Splurging too soon can pull you right back into debt!
goodNote: Steady rebuilding makes your money stronger than before!

title: HABIT RESET
blurb: Old money habits got you stuck? You can swap them out! Today you'll trade harmful habits for helpful ones.
mission: Learn how to spot and change harmful money habits.
what: A money habit is something you do with money again and again.
key: You can swap a harmful habit for a helpful one on purpose.
swapGood: “After school I save my $2 instead of buying snacks.”
intro: Trade a bad habit for a good one…
rule: Catch the habit, then swap it for a better one.
star: One swapped habit can save you $10 a week.
goal: “Swap my $2 daily snack habit for saving it instead.”
smartTip: New habits stick when you do them every day!
gbBody: Some habits help your money grow; others quietly drain it.
gbKey: Swap one harmful habit at a time.
tip: Pause 10 seconds before any unplanned buy.
badWarn: Harmful habits drain your money without you noticing!
goodNote: Helpful habits build your money day by day!

title: FINANCIAL FITNESS
blurb: Strong money habits are like strong muscles — you train them! Today you'll start a money workout that pays off.
mission: Learn to train strong money habits with steady practice, like exercise.
what: Money fitness is keeping strong habits with steady practice.
key: Practice small money moves daily and your habits get strong.
swapGood: “I save $2 every day — that's my daily money workout.”
rule: Small habits done daily build big money strength.
star: Daily reps turn weak habits into strong ones.
goal: “Do my $2 save every day for a 7-day money streak.”
smartTip: Consistency beats intensity — small daily wins add up!
gbBody: Trained habits keep your money strong; lazy ones let it slip.
tip: Pick one money habit and do it at the same time daily.
badWarn: Skipping your habits lets your money strength fade!
goodNote: Trained money habits keep you strong for life!

title: RESILIENCE HALL
blurb: Money setbacks happen to everyone! Today you'll learn to stay calm, learn from it, and keep moving forward.
mission: Learn to stay calm and positive when money setbacks happen.
what: Resilience is staying calm and bouncing back after a setback.
key: A setback is a lesson, not the end — you can always recover.
swapBad: “I messed up, I'll never get money right.”
swapGood: “I lost $5 — okay, what can I learn and do next?”
intro: Turn a setback into a comeback…
rule: Stay calm, learn the lesson, then try again.
star: Every setback is a chance to come back stronger.
goal: “If I lose $5, I'll breathe, learn why, and start saving again.”
smartTip: It's not the slip that counts — it's the comeback!
gbBody: Staying calm helps you recover; panic only makes it worse.
gbKey: A setback is a lesson, not the end.
tip: Take 3 deep breaths before reacting to money news.
badWarn: Panicking makes small setbacks feel huge and stops progress!
goodNote: Calm comebacks make you stronger every time!
